title: Polymorphism, Protocols, and Abstract Contracts · Program Brief: Oop Liskov Contracts
summary: Support interchangeable storage, notification, pricing, and item-policy implementations. Planned artifact: Add and verify the oop liskov contracts increment in a protocol-driven circulation application. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Evaluate subtypes against preconditions, postconditions, invariants, exceptions, and semantic expectations.
Use isinstance, issubclass, runtime-checkable protocols, and explicit capability checks only at justified boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
